{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks whether circular RNAs are implicated in diseases of the eye. None of the retrieved documents from MEDCORP or UMLS mention circular RNAs at all. The documents discuss various eye diseases (e.g., retinal dystrophies, choroidal atrophy, corneal dystrophies, glaucoma), treatments (e.g., gene therapy, siRNA, antisense oligonucleotides, microRNAs), and genetic factors, but there is no reference to circular RNAs or their role in ocular diseases. While some documents mention non-coding RNAs (like microRNA) and siRNA therapies, circular RNAs are not discussed. Therefore, based on the available information, there is no evidence to support a link between circular RNAs and eye diseases.", "answer_choice": "B"}
